args.query: USC Trojan International I-20 request program extension

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Program Extension | Office of International Services

  - url: https://ois.usc.edu/students/maintainingstudentstatus/programextension/

  - source: USC Office of International Services

  - domain: ois.usc.edu


  ## Content


  # Program Extension


  USC explains that F-1 and J-1 students receive a specific period in which to complete
  their academic program. The program end date appears on the student''s Form I-20
  or DS-2019.


  A student who cannot complete the academic program requirements by the current document
  end date must apply for a Program Extension before that end date. Continuous maintenance
  of the relevant student status is part of the eligibility requirements. The delay
  must be supported by an applicable academic or medical reason; when the listed reasons
  do not fit, USC directs the student to consult an OIS advisor.


  For F-1 students, the official process is the Program Extension I-20 Request. USC
  provides a separate request path for Viterbi graduate students. That path requires
  the student to obtain an approved Academic Verification Form through MyViterbi and
  then complete the Program Extension I-20 Request for Viterbi Graduate Students through
  Trojan International.


  The approved Viterbi academic verification supports the request, while OIS performs
  the final review and I-20/SEVIS authorization. The page distinguishes the academic
  verification step from the OIS immigration-document process.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Program Extension | Office of International Services
url: https://ois.usc.edu/students/maintainingstudentstatus/programextension/

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Academic Verifications for International Students

  - url: https://viterbigrad.usc.edu/academic-verifications-for-international-students/

  - source: USC Viterbi Graduate Academic Services

  - domain: viterbigrad.usc.edu


  ## Content


  # Academic Verifications for International Students


  USC Viterbi Graduate Academic Services works with USC''s Office of International
  Services to support F-1 and J-1 students whose immigration processes require academic
  verification.


  Viterbi Graduate Academic Services serves as the academic verifier for international
  Viterbi students. Department academic advisors do not provide the verification on
  Trojan International forms for these students. Academic verification can be required
  for processes including Program Extension, Reduced Course Load, Leave of Absence,
  CPT, and OPT.


  This verification role is limited to the academic portion of the request. Viterbi
  Graduate Academic Services confirms academic information and forwards or supports
  the relevant request, but it does not issue or update Form I-20 or Form DS-2019.
  Authorization and immigration-document processing must be requested from USC''s
  Office of International Services.


  For a Program Extension, the academic unit may verify the student''s expected completion
  timeline and academic progress. That academic verification is an input to the immigration
  process; it is not itself the final I-20 or SEVIS action.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Academic Verifications for International Students
url: https://viterbigrad.usc.edu/academic-verifications-for-international-students/

Your records indicate that this is an **incorrect Form I-20 program-end date**, not a Registrar or Viterbi academic-record problem:

- **Form I-20:** ends **December 15, 2026**
- **Registrar record:** expected completion **May 14, 2027**
- **Viterbi verification:** confirmed completion timeline through **May 14, 2027**
- **Case:** `DOC-2026-0417`
- **Document record:** `DOC-I20-88412`

### USC process to use

Because the mismatch is on the immigration document/SEVIS layer, the responsible process is USC Office of International Services’ **Program Extension I-20 Request**—specifically the **Program Extension I-20 Request for Viterbi Graduate Students** through **Trojan International**.

Your Viterbi academic verification is already confirmed, so use it as the academic-supporting step; Viterbi does not itself update or reissue the I-20. OIS performs the final review and authorizes the I-20/SEVIS update.

### What to do

1. Log in to **Trojan International**.
2. Submit the **Program Extension I-20 Request for Viterbi Graduate Students**.
3. Include/reference your approved Viterbi academic verification and the corrected expected completion date: **May 14, 2027**.
4. Explain that your current Form I-20 shows **December 15, 2026**, although both Viterbi and the Registrar confirm **May 14, 2027**.
5. Contact OIS promptly about your travel plans and ask whether you should wait for the corrected I-20 before traveling.

USC’s guidance says a program extension must be handled **before the current I-20 end date**. Do not assume the discrepancy is resolved until OIS issues the corrected I-20 and confirms the corresponding SEVIS update.

Sources: [USC OIS—Program Extension](https://ois.usc.edu/students/maintainingstudentstatus/programextension/) and [Viterbi—Academic Verifications for International Students](https://viterbigrad.usc.edu/academic-verifications-for-international-students/).

Your records indicate that this is an **incorrect Form I-20 program-end date**, not a Registrar or Viterbi academic-record problem:

- **Form I-20:** ends **December 15, 2026**
- **Registrar record:** expected completion **May 14, 2027**
- **Viterbi verification:** confirmed completion timeline through **May 14, 2027**
- **Case:** `DOC-2026-0417`
- **Document record:** `DOC-I20-88412`

### USC process to use

Because the mismatch is on the immigration document/SEVIS layer, the responsible process is USC Office of International Services’ **Program Extension I-20 Request**—specifically the **Program Extension I-20 Request for Viterbi Graduate Students** through **Trojan International**.

Your Viterbi academic verification is already confirmed, so use it as the academic-supporting step; Viterbi does not itself update or reissue the I-20. OIS performs the final review and authorizes the I-20/SEVIS update.

### What to do

1. Log in to **Trojan International**.
2. Submit the **Program Extension I-20 Request for Viterbi Graduate Students**.
3. Include/reference your approved Viterbi academic verification and the corrected expected completion date: **May 14, 2027**.
4. Explain that your current Form I-20 shows **December 15, 2026**, although both Viterbi and the Registrar confirm **May 14, 2027**.
5. Contact OIS promptly about your travel plans and ask whether you should wait for the corrected I-20 before traveling.

USC’s guidance says a program extension must be handled **before the current I-20 end date**. Do not assume the discrepancy is resolved until OIS issues the corrected I-20 and confirms the corresponding SEVIS update.

Sources: [USC OIS—Program Extension](https://ois.usc.edu/students/maintainingstudentstatus/programextension/) and [Viterbi—Academic Verifications for International Students](https://viterbigrad.usc.edu/academic-verifications-for-international-students/).
